Read More 30 January 2026 Mike Reader MP calls out forced deportation of Ukrainian children at Council of Europe Ukraine 26 January 2026 Police to speed up response times, catch more criminals and protect communities Community 23 January 2026 Mike Reader MP signs Holocaust Educational Trust Book of Commitment Community VIDEOS A GREENER, MORE PROSPEROUS, FAIRER FUTURE. THAT’S MY VISION FOR NORTHAMPTON. I'm standing as Labour and Cooperative candidate in the next general election. Many features are not available without adding an API Key. Please go to the YouTube Feeds settings page to add an API key after following these instructions. IN THE MEDIA 20 January 2025 How the APPGI will drive change in UK government policy in 2025 ICE Engineering can deliver change and strengthen communities, writes Mike Reader MP, new chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Group for Infrastructure. ... 09 January 2025 MP employs worker to deal with Send cases BBC News An MP has appointed a specialist Send (special educational needs and disabilities) caseworker to deal with the “significant volume” of complex cases faced by parents. ... 12 December 2024 Government congratulates University of Northampton on its economic impact Northampton Chronicle The success of the University was raised in the House of Commons, as the local Member of Parliament called for fairer funding for institutions like... 11 December 2024 Minister for Women and Equalities holds vital roundtable with Northamptonshire charities MSN Anneliese Dodds MP, Minister for Women and Equalities, joined Mike Reader, Member of Parliament for Northampton South, and representatives from Northamptonshire’s charitable sector for a... 03 December 2024 Former Mace director Mike Reader to chair All-Party Parliamentary Group on Infrastructure – Water Magazine Water Magazine Mike Reader MP (Labour, Northampton South) has been confirmed as the new chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Group on Infrastructure (APPGI). ... 10 November 2024 Mike Reader MP helps pack Christmas charity boxes with local church Norhamptonshire Telegraph 262 Christmas boxes for children were donated and packed at The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, on Harlestone Road, Duston on Saturday 9th...
